family.mnemonics: Think of 'kazoku' as 'ka-zone-cook.' Imagine your family cooking together in a cozy kitchen zone.

grandfather; old man.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, gee, son!' Picture an old man saying this to his grandson.

grandmother; old woman.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, bah, son!' Imagine a grandmother knitting and saying this.

older brother.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, knee, son!' Imagine an older brother who always kneeled down to tie your shoelaces.

older sister.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Oh, neigh, son!' Imagine an older sister who loves horses and often mimics a horse's neigh.

my father.mnemonics: 'Chichi' sounds like 'chee-chee.' Picture your father playing a game of chess ('chee' for chess).

my mother.mnemonics: Sounds like laughter 'ha-ha.' Remember your mother's laugh.

my older brother.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, knee.' Imagine your older brother always hitting his knee on the table.

my older sister.mnemonics: Sounds like 'Ah, neh.' Imagine your older sister pondering and saying 'Ah, neh' when thinking.

younger sister.mnemonics: Sounds like 'E-moto.' Picture your younger sister riding an e-motorbike.
